title: Work
short: Energy transferred by a force acting over a distance, A = F·s, measured in joules (J).
full:
Work is done when a force moves an object through a displacement: A = F·s for a constant force along the direction of motion. It is the mechanism by which energy is transferred — doing positive work on an object adds energy to it.

If the force is perpendicular to the motion it does no work (carrying a bag horizontally does no work against gravity). The work–energy theorem states that the net work equals the change in kinetic energy.

The SI unit is the joule (J); 1 J = 1 N·m.
